title: Styphelia gracillima
intro: Styphelia gracillima, also known as the Tasmanian button-sedge, is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the Ericaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve congestion and soothe the respiratory tract. Styphelia gracillima contains bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Styphelia gracillima is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a decoction, or dried and used as a powder or capsule supplement.
